summary: Matched the common-fighter Mewtwo capture/thrown routines by replacing a block of assembly in asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s with new C files for ftCo_CaptureMewtwo and ftCo_ThrownMewtwo. The PR added the new objects to obj_files.mk, introduced headers for the newly decompiled symbols, and factored a shared thrown-state entry helper into ftCommon/inlines.h for reuse by both ThrownKoopa and ThrownMewtwo code. The generated report shows 100% matching for ftCo_800BCF18, ftCo_800BD000, ftCo_800BD0E8, and the empty Mewtwo thrown callbacks.
what_changed: Removed the assembly implementations for ftCo_800BCF18, ftCo_800BD000, ftCo_800BD0E8, ftCo_ThrownMewtwo_Anim, ftCo_ThrownMewtwo_IASA, ftCo_ThrownMewtwo_Phys, ftCo_ThrownMewtwo_Coll, and their Air variants from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureMewtwo.c containing a shared static inline doEnter helper used by ftCo_800BCF18 and ftCo_800BD000.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ThrownMewtwo.c containing ftCo_800BD0E8 plus no-op callback functions for ground and air thrown-Mewtwo Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll states.
Added headers ftCo_CaptureMewtwo.h and ftCo_ThrownMewtwo.h with address comments and prototypes for the new symbols.
Added ftCo_CaptureMewtwo.c.o and ftCo_ThrownMewtwo.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_08A4 assembly object.
Moved the thrown-entry helper pattern from a file-local inlineA0 in ftCo_ThrownKoopa.c into a reusable ftCo_Thrown_Enter inline in ftCommon/inlines.h.
Updated ftCo_ThrownKoopa.c to include ftCommon/inlines.h and call ftCo_Thrown_Enter in place of the former local inlineA0 helper.
Removed four sdata2 constants from ft_08A4.s corresponding to zero/one float literals used by the newly matched C code.
smart_moves: Used a local doEnter helper in ftCo_CaptureMewtwo.c to share the nearly identical ground and air capture setup paths; the only differing inputs are the capturer and victim motion IDs.

Factored the repeated thrown-state setup sequence into ftCo_Thrown_Enter so future common thrown states can reuse the same facing-dir synchronization, capturekoopa flag clear, Fighter_ChangeMotionState call, accessory callback assignment, and ftCommon_8007E2F4 call.
Kept the Mewtwo thrown callbacks as explicit empty functions rather than leaving them in assembly, matching the original blr-only routines with minimal C.
Used address-commented prototypes in the new headers, consistent with the project’s decomp style for still-numbered symbols.
Preserved matching-sensitive workaround structure in ftCo_ThrownMewtwo.c with SOLUTION selection and MUST_MATCH unused-stack padding instead of forcing the cleaner inline when it did not match.
Updated build ordering in obj_files.mk so the newly decompiled C object files replace only the removed assembly range while keeping the rest of ft_08A4.s assembled.
decomp_lessons: When two action-state entry functions differ only by motion IDs, a static inline helper can reduce duplication while still matching, as shown by doEnter for ftCo_800BCF18 and ftCo_800BD000.

Shared inline helpers should be promoted to ftCommon/inlines.h when the same register/order-sensitive pattern appears across multiple common fighter files, but callers may still need fallback hand-written code for exact matching.
Empty assembly callbacks can be safely represented as empty C functions when the report confirms 4-byte 100% matches.
Matching action-state transitions often depends on preserving the exact sequence around fighter flags, facing_dir, victim_gobj, callback assignment, and Fighter_ChangeMotionState arguments.
Conditional MUST_MATCH stack padding remains useful for functions with unused stack artifacts; this PR kept u8 _[8] = { 0 } in matching-sensitive paths.

For thrown/capture code, synchronizing fp->facing_dir from GET_FIGHTER(fp->victim_gobj)->facing_dir and passing fp->victim_gobj through Fighter_ChangeMotionState are recurring patterns worth checking before inventing new struct fields or control flow.
naming_conventions: New files follow the ftCommon action naming pattern: ftCo_CaptureMewtwo.c/.h and ftCo_ThrownMewtwo.c/.h.
Public callbacks use descriptive action names such as ftCo_ThrownMewtwo_Anim, ftCo_ThrownMewtwo_IASA, ftCo_ThrownMewtwo_Phys, ftCo_ThrownMewtwo_Coll, with Air variants named ftCo_ThrownMewtwoAir_*.
Unknown or not-yet-renamed functions retain address-style names such as ftCo_800BCF18, ftCo_800BD000, and ftCo_800BD0E8.
Header guards use GALE01 address identifiers, e.g. GALE01_0BCF18 and GALE01_0BD17C.
Shared helper naming was improved from the vague local inlineA0 to ftCo_Thrown_Enter, reflecting its role in entering thrown states.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced 202 deleted assembly lines from ft_08A4.s with matched C while leaving later ft_08A4 assembly symbols in place.
The report excerpt shows ftCo_CaptureMewtwo.c matched 464 bytes at 100%, including ftCo_800BCF18 and ftCo_800BD000 at 232 bytes each.
The report excerpt shows ftCo_ThrownMewtwo.c matched 180 bytes at 100%, including ftCo_800BD0E8 at 148 bytes and eight 4-byte empty callbacks.
Used inline helpers for register/order shaping: static inline doEnter for capture setup and static inline ftCo_Thrown_Enter for common thrown setup.

Kept a SOLUTION macro in ftCo_ThrownMewtwo.c: the direct ftCo_Thrown_Enter call is documented as SOLUTION 0, while SOLUTION 1 expands the code manually with MUST_MATCH stack padding for the matching build.
Preserved exact motion flag behavior in ftCo_800BD0E8 with the conditional expression on fp->x2222_b6 and explicit Fighter_ChangeMotionState call.
Assigned fp->cb.x21B0_callback_Accessory1 with an HSD_GObjEvent cast to ftCo_800DE508 in the shared helper and manual matching path.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the supplied PR context.

The diff itself contains TODO-style notes documenting matching limitations: ftCo_800BD0E8 has a TODO for SOLUTION 0 using ftCo_Thrown_Enter, and ftCo_ThrownKoopa.c retains a TODO around using ftCo_Thrown_Enter in one matching-sensitive branch.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftCo_800BCF18, ftCo_800BD000, or ftCo_800BD0E8 to semantic capture/thrown Mewtwo names.
Search for additional callers or replacements of ftCo_Thrown_Enter to see whether the helper became broadly reusable.
Investigate whether the SOLUTION 0 path in ftCo_ThrownMewtwo.c was ever enabled after compiler or struct improvements.
Compare other capture/thrown common-fighter states for the same doEnter pattern and motion flag ordering.
